title: Cozy Malty Saudi Falafel Bowl
description: A warm, nut-free bowl of falafel with toasted malted barley and a silky tahini lemon sauce that brings the comfort of Saudi flavors to a bowl.
instructions: In a large bowl combine drained chickpeas, chopped onion, crushed garlic, chopped cilantro, cumin, coriander, paprika, and baking soda until evenly mixed.
Using a mortar and pestle pound the mixture until it forms a coarse paste that holds together, adding a splash of water if needed.
Shape the paste into small balls about walnut size and heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Fry the falafel balls in batches, turning until golden brown on all sides, about 3 minutes per side, then transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
While falafel cooks, toast the malted barley grains in a dry skillet over medium heat until lightly browned and fragrant.
Whisk tahini with lemon juice and a splash of water until smooth and pourable, seasoning with a pinch of salt.
Assemble the bowl by layering cilantro, adding the fried falafel, sprinkling toasted barley, and drizzling the tahini sauce.
Garnish with extra lemon wedges if desired and serve warm.
